author: @jeffreybaker4399
text: There was a time when the commissioners of the major sports were men who had had a life. Men who had struggled and made a name for themselves outside the corporate sports structure. Today, what we have are guys who went to college, got their degree in law, and very early on got a job within the league they now preside over. They rubbed elbows with the owners and the owners thought, "now there's a good company man". The league's corporate culture is all they know and their goal is increasing quarterly profits for that corporation. Scheme's over quality. Projected new markets over serving the fan base. Like a fair number of CEOs in other businesses, the commissioners don't necessarily understand their customers and have no other life experience to fall back on.

author: @PristineTX
text:
The advertisers are going to request “make goods” for ratings numbers that were about half of what was expected for the show. This is common practice, especially for live events that don’t come close to delivering an expected number. 

The network will give those to the advertisers, as this is industry practice, and that money will come out of the network’s payment to the NFL, which will reduce the team owners’ disbursements. 

Team owners don’t like losing out on the full amount they earn on their version of “Black Friday.” And worse, the idea of a competitive event will probably become an annual thing, especially if Jay-Z remains the booker for the halftime show.

It may be different in Puerto Rico, but there is a reason why radio station broadcast clean versions of songs because it has objectionable words that won’t get past FCC and our decency laws and someone showed a image of the back up dancers and let’s say they look like they were doing stimulated adult things to each other and that is a no no remember they band Janet Jackson from ever performing live TV again because her wardrobe malfunction the NFL commissioner is going to go guarantee you what will happen in short order right now. All of the owners are calling each other talking and if you’re yearly meeting, which should be soon I’m willing to bet the subject of the Super Bowl was going to come up and the fact that 40% of all NFL players do not like the way that Mr. Godell is taking NFL and given the fact that he went on stage and hugged the man. He likes this kind of thing and they’re tired of the woke the NFL has been hurting and it’s been hurting since Colin Cabnet decided to be stupid enough to take a knee and then the NFL commissioner, letting the players do that and then playing things like the black national anthem and all this other crap his choices are bad and he’s about to suffer the consequence.
